meaning: n.绞死,绞刑,悬挂,(通常用复数)帘子、幔帐
examples: He faced execution by hanging for murder.
Both these facts strengthen the case against hanging.
The dog's tongue was hanging out.
Taride spent his teenage years hanging around the beach and dreaming of being a musician.
Another artist joked morbidly that one day Picasso would be found hanging behind "his giant canvas".
